mcq.q: What type of work does the job mainly involve?

1.errorText: A man called Peter Chin phones King's Restaurant about a job vacancy. The work mainly involves washing up, and not waiting at tables. The restaurant has two branches, and the one recruiting is on Hillsdunne Street. The nearest bus stop is beside the post office. The pay is £3.95 an hour. Staff get extra pay for working on public holidays, and anyone working after midnight gets a free lift home. The restaurant is looking for someone with a loud voice who can think quickly. Peter arranges to come for an interview on Thursday 22nd November at 6 o'clock, and is told to ask for Samira.

signpost.quote: When you first start working somewhere you will realise that the organisation you've joined has certain characteristics — we often refer to these as the culture of the organisation.
opts: Defining the key concept before introducing the classification

signpost.quote: But on the negative side, this type of organisation doesn't always act effectively, because it depends too much on one or two people at the top.
opts: Introducing a limitation after listing the benefit

signpost.quote: Moving on now to Task Cultures — this type is found in organisations that are project-oriented.

signpost.quote: One of the major benefits of this culture is that it's flexible. But it does have some major disadvantages too.

signpost.quote: And finally, the fourth category is called the Person Culture.
opts: Signalling the last in a series of four categories

dictation.speaker: LECTURER
parts: When you first start working somewhere you'll realise that the organisation has certain
. We often refer to these as the culture of the organisation.
One well-known writer has classified company cultures by identifying four major
The first type is the Power Culture, usually found in small organisations. Control is in the hands of just one or two people — there aren't many
or procedures.
Communication usually takes the form of
rather than formal meetings. One benefit is that the organisation can act very
But this type depends too much on the people at the top — when they make poor
, there's no-one else who can influence them.
The next type is Role Culture — found in large companies with lots of different
. Everyone's job is controlled by rules and procedures.
One benefit: it can achieve economies of
. But it's often very slow to recognise the need for change, and even slower to
Task Culture is found in project-oriented organisations. Management delegates the projects and other
. Little day-to-day control is exercised from the top.
One major benefit of this culture is that it's
. But it can't produce economies of scale or great depth of expertise.
Finally, the fourth category is the Person Culture. Here the individual is the central point, and this type is typically found in professional firms like
or accountancy.

1.errorText: A caller contacts Greek Island Holidays to ask about apartments in Arilas, Corfu. The Rose Garden Apartments have a studio flat available in the last week of October, at £219 each. The Blue Bay apartments feature a large freshwater swimming pool and are 300 metres from the beach. The Sunshade Apartments are on a hillside, each room has its own sun terrace, and facilities include water sports and satellite TV. Each person must pay £490 and there must be at least three in an apartment. The Grand is an older house with Greek paintings in every room and a terrace. The price is £390.

The owners — both professionals, but not
— wanted to be closely involved, so they decided to manage the project themselves.
Their chief aim was to create somewhere as environmentally-friendly as possible. They also wanted to live somewhere
— they'd both grown up in a rural area and disliked urban life.
They found a disused stone
in a beautiful area. The price was low and they liked the idea of
the land.
The house was built into the earth itself with two
. The north, east and west sides were set in the earth; only the south-facing side was exposed to light — made of a double layer of very strong
There were photovoltaic
fixed to the sloping wall — designed to store energy from the sun. The walls also had a layer of foam for extra
Sunlight floods in through the glass wall. To maximise it there are lots of mirrors and windows inside — this helps to spread the
around. That's the first energy-saving feature.
In addition, the special tiles on the outside convert energy from the sun and generate some of the house's
The floors are made of reclaimed
. The owners haven't bought a single item of new furniture.
Waste is dealt with
— purified by being filtered through reed beds planted in the
The construction released 70 tons of carbon dioxide. However, once this initial 'debt' has been cleared — calculated at only fifteen
— the house costs nothing environmentally.

1.errorText: A man calls about registering with a doctor at a health centre after moving to the area with his wife and children. The receptionist first recommends Doctor Green at The Harvey Clinic, who specialises in care for teenagers. The next option is the Eshcol Health Practice with Doctor Baker, which only opened a year ago so facilities are very modern. It also offers appointments in the morning. The Shore Lane Health Centre has Doctor Gormley, who is new there. The vaccination service for travel abroad is free, but sports injury treatment and nutritional therapy are paid services. The talk on healthy eating takes place at the secondary school on Shore Lane.

signpost.quote: I'll start by saying something about the background to the project, then talk a little bit about our research techniques, and then indicate some of our interim findings.
opts: Providing a clear roadmap for the structure of the presentation

signpost.quote: Our own informal discussions with neighbours and friends led us to believe that many garden owners had interesting experiences regarding wild animal sightings.
opts: Explaining how informal observation informed the formal research design

signpost.quote: So what did we find? Well, so much that I just won't have time to tell you about here.
opts: Signalling the transition to results while managing audience expectations

signpost.quote: What we've decided to present today is information about just three species — because we felt these gave a good indication of the processes at work.
opts: Narrowing the scope of what will be covered and justifying the selection

signpost.quote: Another factor is the provision of nesting places — which is actually better in gardens than the countryside. Hard to believe it, but it's true.
opts: Introducing a counterintuitive finding to reinforce a point

dictation.speaker: RESEARCHER
parts: Today I'd like to present the findings of our Year 2 project on wildlife found in
throughout our city.
One day while we were discussing a possible focus, two of the group had seen sparrow-hawks — one of Britain's most interesting birds of
— in their own city centre gardens.
We estimated that about one fifth of urban land is taken up by private gardens, and this was confirmed by looking at large-scale usage
— 24% to be precise.
Just over 100 garden owners completed a
once every two weeks for twelve months, ticking off species they had seen from a pro forma list.
We deliberately chose
gardens because they were by far the most typical in the city. The whole point was to look at the norm, not the exception.
The first species was
. There was a clear pattern — they proliferate where there is suitable water. Garden ponds are on the increase, rural ponds are disappearing — leading to massive migration to the towns.
Hedgehogs are also finding it easier to live in urban areas — this time because their
are not finding it attractive to leave their rural environment.
Hedgehogs have a better
rate in cities. We had lots of sightings and no difficulties counting their numbers precisely.
Our final species is the song thrush. On the decline in the countryside, they are experiencing a
in urban gardens because gardeners are buying lots of different plants with a wide range of
— which is what they feed on.
Another factor is the provision of
places — which is actually better in gardens than in the countryside.
A massive new
on song thrushes is about to be launched, so keep an eye open for that.
